_meta.source: America's Health Rankings (United Health Foundation), sourced from EPA Safe Drinking Water Information System (SDWIS) via ECHO
_meta.notes: Average number of health-based Safe Drinking Water Act violations per community water system, by state. Lower is better (fewer violations). National average ~2.5. This is a per-system rate, not a population-weighted 'share of residents affected' figure — a state with many small rural systems can score worse here even if population impact is small. EPA does not publish a ready-made state-level population-weighted equivalent; only national/EPA-Region rollups exist (EPA Report on the Environment, indicator #45). Values extracted from AHR's per-state pages (2026-07); spot-checked HI (0.0, rank 1/50) and RI (5.5, rank 50/50) against the live site.
